# out/ Compiled Output Directory

## Overview

`out/` is a directory auto-generated by the TypeScript compiler (`tsc`) from `src/`.
**Do not edit manually.**

## How to Build

```bash
# One-time build
npm run compile

# Watch for file changes and auto-build
npm run watch
```

## Generated Files

The following are generated for each `.ts` file in `src/`:

| Generated File | Source File | Description |
|----------------|-------------|-------------|
| `*.js` | `src/*.ts` | JavaScript compiled to CommonJS format |
| `*.js.map` | `src/*.ts` | Source maps (for debugging) |

Current file list:
- `extension.js` / `.map`
- `pomodoroTimer.js` / `.map`
- `hijackPanel.js` / `.map`
- `breakTemplate.js` / `.map`
- `settings.js` / `.map`
- `types.js` / `.map`
- `constants.js` / `.map`
- `utils.js` / `.map`

## Compiler Settings

Key settings in `tsconfig.json`:

| Setting | Value | Description |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| `target` | ES2022 | Output JS version |
| `module` | commonjs | Module system |
| `outDir` | `out` | Output directory |
| `rootDir` | `src` | Source directory |
| `sourceMap` | true | Generate source maps for debugging |
| `strict` | true | TypeScript strict mode |

## Notes

- `out/` is excluded from version control via `.gitignore`
- Referenced as `"main": "./out/extension.js"` in the VSCode `package.json`
- The `vscode:prepublish` script automatically compiles before publishing
